hypothesis: Feature-pairing heuristics for tabular anomaly detection can be evaluated under the shared benchmark protocol without changing the QA state alphabet.

null_model.description: Benchmark baselines and dataset splits are declared in benchmark_protocol.json; Spearman rank correlation is used only as an observer-side feature-pairing heuristic.
null_model.generating_process: Compute feature-pairing statistics on the training split only, then compare against benchmark outputs declared in benchmark_protocol.json.
null_model.independence_argument: The pairing statistic is computed on the training split only and does not use anomaly labels from the test split.

decision_rules.accept_criterion: Variant results are interpreted through the parity and metrics declared in benchmark_protocol.json.
decision_rules.reject_criterion: Reject any variant that violates the benchmark parity contract or lacks comparable AUROC/runtime output.

observer_projection.description: Training-only feature pairing followed by quantile binning onto (b,e) in {1..24}^2.

source_mapping.mapping_rationale: The experiment only evaluates observer-side pairing after the benchmark protocol has fixed the datasets, baselines, and calibration provenance.

ablation.destroyed_structure: shuffle feature-pair to QA-state assignments before scoring
ablation.expected_direction: feature-pairing advantage should disappear or move toward the benchmark null
